title: cyperus calderoniae
intro: Cyperus calderoniae, also known as Calderon's sedge or Calderonia's papyrus, is a species of sedge native to the Philippines. It is a type of aquatic plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus calderoniae include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used in infusions, decoctions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
